alignments.preprint_claim_text: We demonstrated feasibility of navigation using inverse reinforcement learning (IRL). (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result with the same focus and certainty, simply adding the MT context; meaning and strength are preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Both single and dual device tracking methods achieved high success rates of 95% and 96%, respectively. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the numerical success rates and context (guidewire navigation), preserving meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A success rate of 100% and procedure time of 22.6 s were obtained when training with a reward function obtained through reward shaping. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results underscore the potential of using reward shaping to train models, offering a promising avenue for enhancing the accessibility and precision of mechanical thrombectomy. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results indicate that reward shaping outperformed both a dense reward function and an IRL-derived reward function in terms of success rate and procedure time. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint made a general claim about potential improvements in accessibility/precision; the publication revises this to a direct comparative performance claim (outperforming alternatives), narrowing to measured metrics and strengthening the implication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Future research can extend our methodology to diverse anatomical structures to enhance generalizability. (certainty=low, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include the forward-looking generalizability suggestion present in the preprint; this future-work claim is omitted.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The study contributes to the advancement of autonomous endovascular intervention navigation by effectively employing IRL based on demonstrator expertise. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This explicit contribution statement (employing IRL based on demonstrator expertise) is not present in the preprint as a standalone claim and appears as an added framing in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed lightweight neural network can represent different types of woven fabrics at different scales.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The network achieves rendering and editing of woven fabrics at nearly 60 frames per second on an RTX 3090.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The network achieves rendering and editing of woven fabrics at nearly 60 frames per second on an RTX 3090.
alignments.rationale: The performance claim is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method shows a quality close to the ground truth and is free from visible aliasing and noise.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method shows a quality close to the ground truth and is free from visible aliasing and noise.
alignments.rationale: The quality/result claim is the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Non-symmetric models can easily learn symmetries from data, which can be beneficial for the accuracy of the model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There are indications that unconstrained models can easily learn symmetries from data, and that doing so can even be beneficial for the accuracy of the model.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same observation about unconstrained/non-symmetric models learning symmetries and benefits to accuracy with equivalent meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A model that obeys rotational invariance only approximately shows negligible consequences in an interpolative, bulk regime.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Negligible consequences are found when the model is used in an interpolative, bulk regime regarding non-invariant behavior under rotations.
alignments.rationale: This result about negligible effects in the interpolative bulk regime is restated in the publication with the same scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model remains very stable for extrapolative gas-phase predictions, despite noticeable symmetry artifacts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model remains very stable for extrapolative gas-phase predictions, even though symmetry artifacts are noticeable.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this performance claim, only slightly rephrasing the clause about symmetry artifacts.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Strategies can be used to systematically reduce the magnitude of symmetry breaking, impacting the convergence of observables.
alignments.rationale: The specific claim about strategies to systematically reduce symmetry breaking and effects on convergence does not appear explicitly in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: An unconstrained architecture can be trained to achieve a high degree of rotational invariance.
alignments.rationale: This explicit statement about achieving rotational invariance via an unconstrained architecture is present in the publication but was not in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Incorporating symmetries constrains the model design space and implies more complicated architectures that are often computationally demanding.
alignments.rationale: The published commentary about the cost/complexity of incorporating symmetries is new relative to the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our multimodal learning-based method synthesizes co-speech facial expressions and upper-body gestures for digital characters using RGB video data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our multimodal learning-based method synthesizes co-speech facial expressions and upper-body gestures for digital characters using RGB video data.
alignments.rationale: The core contribution claim is retained verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method generates plausible emotive character motions by learning from sparse face landmarks and upper-body joints estimated from video data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method generates plausible emotive character motions by learning from sparse face landmarks and upper-body joints estimated from video data.
alignments.rationale: The result about generating plausible emotive motions is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach results in low reconstruction error and produces synthesized samples with diverse facial expressions and body gestures.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach results in low reconstruction error and produces synthesized samples with diverse facial expressions and body gestures.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the claims about reconstruction error and diversity of synthesized samples.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We enhance the plausibility of synthesis using an adversarial discriminator that differentiates between original and synthesized motions based on affective expressions.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include the adversarial discriminator as a claimed component; instead it emphasizes evaluation, so this methodological claim appears omitted.

alignments.pub_claim_text: We evaluate our method through quantitative and qualitative experiments on multiple evaluation metrics and via a user study.
alignments.rationale: The explicit mention of the evaluation suite and a user study is present in the publication but was not stated as a claim in the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing methods for unsupervised sequential disentanglement often suffer from information leakage, leading to non-disentangled representations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing methods for unsupervised sequential disentanglement often suffer from information leakage, leading to non-disentangled representations.
alignments.rationale: The limitation claim is the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Attempts to alleviate information leakage through reducing dynamic dimensions and auxiliary loss terms achieve only partial success.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Attempts to alleviate information leakage through reducing dynamic dimensions and auxiliary loss terms achieve only partial success.
alignments.rationale: The characterization of prior attempts and their limited success is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed architecture mitigates information leakage by employing a subtraction inductive bias while conditioning on a single sample.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed architecture mitigates information leakage by employing a subtraction inductive bias while conditioning on a single sample.
alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is preserved unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The resulting variational framework is simpler in terms of required loss terms, hyperparameters, and data augmentation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The resulting variational framework is simpler in terms of required loss terms, hyperparameters, and data augmentation.
alignments.rationale: The claim about simplicity of the framework remains the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method achieves state-of-the-art results on generation and prediction tasks across multiple data-modality benchmarks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method achieves state-of-the-art results on generation and prediction tasks across multiple data-modality benchmarks.
alignments.rationale: The performance/result claim is unchanged between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Generalized Additive Models (GAMs) can explain deep neural networks (DNNs) at the feature level.
alignments.rationale: The publication's claims do not include any statement that GAMs explain DNNs at the feature level; that preprint claim is absent in the final paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed CAT model does not require domain experts to annotate concepts and their ground-truth values.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CAT, a novel interpretable Concept-bAsed Taylor additive model, simplifies the process of concept annotation by not requiring domain experts to label concepts and their ground-truth values.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution about removing the need for domain experts to annotate concepts and ground-truth values with equivalent emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Evaluation results across multiple benchmarks demonstrate that CAT can outperform or compete with the baselines while reducing the need for extensive model parameters.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Evaluation results across multiple benchmarks demonstrate that CAT can outperform or compete with the baselines while reducing the need for extensive model parameters.
alignments.rationale: The evaluation/result claim appears verbatim in the publication with no substantive change in scope or confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CAT can explain model predictions through high-level concepts that humans can understand.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CAT can effectively explain model predictions through high-level concepts.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the core claim about explaining predictions via high-level concepts but tightens wording (drops explicit "humans can understand" and adds "effectively"), a minor clarification rather than substantive revision.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The SASSP method generates adversarial examples that maintain high semantic fidelity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The SASSP method generates adversarial examples that maintain high semantic fidelity while effectively deceiving state-of-the-art natural language processing models.
alignments.rationale: The publication merges the fidelity claim with deception performance into a single statement; the fidelity assertion itself is preserved but presented together with deception capability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SASSP effectively deceives state-of-the-art natural language processing models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The SASSP method generates adversarial examples that maintain high semantic fidelity while effectively deceiving state-of-the-art natural language processing models.
alignments.rationale: The publication combines deception and fidelity in one sentence, but the claim that SASSP deceives SOTA models is explicitly present and unchanged in meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SASSP yields a higher attack success rate compared to the original scheme of contextual perturbation CLARE.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SASSP has yielded a higher attack success rate compared to the original scheme of contextual perturbation CLARE.
alignments.rationale: The attack-success-rate comparison to CLARE is carried forward into the publication with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SASSP achieves a lower word perturbation rate than the original scheme of contextual perturbation CLARE.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include an explicit claim about reduced word perturbation rate relative to CLARE; that specific metric-based claim was omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The SAPPhIRE causality model is useful in supporting design-by-analogy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The SAPPhIRE causality model is useful in supporting design-by-analogy.
alignments.rationale: The publication reiterates the same conclusion about the usefulness of SAPPhIRE for design-by-analogy with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Creating a SAPPhIRE model of artificial or biological systems is an effort-intensive process that requires human experts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Creating a SAPPhIRE model of artificial or biological systems is an effort-intensive process that requires human experts.
alignments.rationale: The limitation about effort and requirement for human experts appears unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This research investigates leveraging Large Language Models (LLMs) to create structured descriptions of systems using the SAPPhIRE model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This research presents a new Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) tool for generating information related to SAPPhIRE constructs of artificial systems.
alignments.rationale: The preprint framed the work as an investigation into leveraging LLMs, while the publication states a concrete presented tool (RAG) — a stronger, more definite contribution, broadening and amplifying the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A new Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) tool for generating information related to SAPPhIRE constructs of artificial systems has been developed.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This research presents a new Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) tool for generating information related to SAPPhIRE constructs of artificial systems.
alignments.rationale: The development/presentation of the RAG tool is retained directly in the publication with equivalent emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The preliminary evaluation of the tool focuses on the factual accuracy and reliability of outcomes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper reports results from a preliminary evaluation of the tool’s success, focusing on factual accuracy and reliability of outcomes.
alignments.rationale: The preliminary evaluation claim and its focus on factual accuracy and reliability are reported similarly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Finsta enhances video-language models (VLMs) with a fine-grained structural spatio-temporal alignment learning method.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Finsta enhances video-language models (VLMs) with a fine-grained structural spatio-temporal alignment learning method.
alignments.rationale: The core contribution about Finsta's spatio-temporal alignment method is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Finsta improves the existing 13 strong-performing VLMs consistently across 6 representative VL modeling tasks over 12 datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Finsta improves the existing 13 strong-performing VLMs consistently across 6 representative VL modeling tasks over 12 datasets.
alignments.rationale: The empirical claim about consistent improvement across the specified models, tasks, and datasets remains the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Finsta refreshes the current state-of-the-art end task performance significantly in both fine-tuning and zero-shot settings.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Finsta refreshes the current state-of-the-art end task performance significantly in both fine-tuning and zero-shot settings.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same result claim regarding state-of-the-art performance improvements.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method is designed as a plug&play system that can be integrated into existing well-trained VLMs without training from scratch.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method can be integrated into existing well-trained VLMs for further representation augmentation without training from scratch.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the integration/plug-and-play characterization but refocuses wording toward representation augmentation; this is a mild clarification rather than a substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Unsupervised Domain Adaptation (UDA) provides a promising solution for cross-domain 3D object detection.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Unsupervised Domain Adaptation (UDA) provides a promising solution for cross-domain 3D object detection.
alignments.rationale: The high-level conclusion about UDA as a promising solution is stated the same way in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Self-Training (ST) based cross-domain 3D detection methods face the issue of low-quality pseudo-labels due to significant domain disparities.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Self-Training (ST) based cross-domain 3D detection methods face the issue of low-quality pseudo-labels due to significant domain disparities.
alignments.rationale: The limitation regarding low-quality pseudo-labels from ST methods is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed STAL3D framework effectively combines Self-Training and Adversarial Learning to improve 3D object detection across domains.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed STAL3D framework effectively combines Self-Training and Adversarial Learning to improve 3D object detection across domains.
alignments.rationale: The described contribution of STAL3D as combining ST and adversarial learning is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: STAL3D achieves state-of-the-art performance on multiple cross-domain tasks, surpassing Oracle results on specific datasets.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the state-of-the-art / surpassing-Oracle claim but omits the explicit dataset enumeration from the preprint, narrowing the specificity of the scope.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper presents a comprehensive survey of existing deep learning-based methods for estimating depth from single RGB images and videos. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication claim text are effectively identical in scope and wording, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The survey provides a taxonomy for classifying current work based on input and output modalities, network architectures, and learning methods. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The survey provides a taxonomy for classifying current work based on input and output modalities, network architectures, and learning methods. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The taxonomy claim is unchanged between preprint and publication with identical description and emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper discusses major milestones in the history of monocular depth estimation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper discusses the major milestones in the history of monocular depth estimation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both versions state the same conclusion about discussing historical milestones with no substantive wording change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The development of digital twins increasingly leverages artificial intelligence for combining data from different sources. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The development of digital twins increasingly leverages artificial intelligence for combining data from different sources. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution statement is carried over verbatim between preprint and publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Current development practices for digital twins are ad-hoc, making the design of AI pipelines complex and time-consuming. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The limitation statement is unchanged in the published version.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Function+Data Flow (FDF) is proposed as a domain-specific language to describe AI pipelines within digital twins. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The main contribution (FDF DSL) is present and unchanged in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: FDF aims to facilitate the design and validation of digital twins by treating functions as first-class citizens. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The benefits of FDF are illustrated through two use cases: predicting plastic strain and modeling electromagnetic behavior. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The benefits of FDF are illustrated through use cases in predicting plastic strain and modeling electromagnetic behavior. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The use-case examples and their roles illustrating benefits are the same; publication wording is slightly rephrased but meaning is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework enables non-experts to articulate task requirements to the system through a chat interface. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Connecting a large language model agent to the robot operating system enables a versatile framework for embodied intelligence. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The published claim preserves the high-level framework goal but omits the specific assertion about non-experts using a chat interface, so the claim is revised and somewhat narrowed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive experiments validate the framework, showcasing robustness, scalability, and versatility in diverse scenarios. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experiments validate the framework, showcasing robustness, scalability, and versatility in diverse scenarios and embodiments. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework supports three behavior modes: sequence, behavior tree, and state machine. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Imitation learning is utilized for adding new robot actions to the library of possible actions. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The published claims do not mention imitation learning for action addition; that specific methodological claim is absent in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The code for the framework has been made open-source to facilitate adoption and reproduction of results. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication does not state that the framework code was released open-source; instead it mentions use of open-source pretrained LLMs, so the preprint's claim about code release is not present.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The results presented in this work were achieved by utilizing open-source pretrained large language models. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Recursive Multi-Branch Information Fusion Network (RMFNet) separates positive and negative events for complementary information extraction. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication's claims concern a different framework (CATI) and do not mention RMFNet or event separation, so the preprint contribution is not present in the published claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate that our approach achieves over 17% and 31% improvement on synthetic and real datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents evaluations for a different method and does not report the stated improvements, so this result claim is absent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The RMFNet method provides a 2.3X acceleration compared to existing methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: No publication claim corresponds to the reported acceleration; the published paper focuses on a different approach.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method achieves remarkable results in object recognition and video reconstruction, outperforming existing methods. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published claims do not reference these object recognition or video reconstruction results; thus this preprint claim is removed.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The Concept-Augmented Textual Inversion (CATI) framework effectively integrates new object information from user-provided concept videos. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces CATI and concept-integration claims that do not appear in the preprint's claims, so these are added publication claims.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The Dual Prior Supervision (DPS) mechanism improves the fidelity of novel concepts by stabilizing editing results and preventing undesired changes to non-target areas. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: This DPS mechanism and its effects are new publication claims not present in the preprint claims list.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive evaluations demonstrate that the proposed solution outperforms state-of-the-art methods in generating lifelike and stable edited videos. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The published evaluation result pertains to CATI and is not reflected among the preprint's claims, so it is an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Counterfactual reasoning can be explored using a mathematical framework based on vector autoregressive (VAR) processes. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Counterfactual reasoning can be explored using a mathematical framework based on vector autoregressive (VAR) processes. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The contribution statement about VAR-based counterfactual reasoning is the same in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The inference of a causal model can be formulated as a joint regression task using both data with and without interventions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The inference of a causal model can be formulated as a joint regression task using both data with and without interventions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The formulation of model inference as a joint regression task is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The VAR model allows for exact predictions about the effects of counterfactual interventions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The linearity of the VAR model allows for exact predictions about the effects of counterfactual interventions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies that the linearity property of the VAR model is the reason exact predictions are possible; otherwise the result claim is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The total causal effects of past counterfactual interventions can be quantified using the proposed framework. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The total causal effects of past counterfactual interventions can be quantified. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The quantification of total causal effects is present in both versions with no substantive change beyond minor wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: LLMs have higher accuracy in object and activity annotations than in emotion and genre annotations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: LLMs have higher accuracy in object and activity annotations than in emotion and genre annotations. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication texts are effectively identical in content and certainty, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study identifies both the potential and limitations of LLM's capabilities in annotating videos. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study identifies both the potential and limitations of LLM's capabilities in annotating videos. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The claim about potential and limitations is stated the same way in both versions with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The research explores opportunities and challenges for future research and improvements to the LLM-assisted multimodal content analysis workflow. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A new workflow for LLM-assisted multimodal content analysis was developed, encompassing codebook design, prompt engineering, LLM processing, and human evaluation. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication replaces a broad exploration of opportunities/challenges with a concrete developed workflow (more specific), increasing certainty; this clarifies and narrows the original, higher-certainty claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Encoding multiple features together in a single input determines their relative importance, thus limiting the network's ability to learn from the data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Encoding multiple features together in a single input determines their relative importance, thus limiting the network’s ability to learn from the data. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Text and certainty are equivalent between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: By selecting an optimal encoding method, we achieve up to a 12.3% improvement in accuracy of PNNs trained on the Iris dataset compared to other encoding techniques. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: By selecting an optimal encoding method, we achieve up to a 12.3% improvement in the accuracy of PNNs trained on the Iris dataset compared to other encoding techniques. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings highlight the importance of carefully choosing the encoding to the accuracy and decision-making strategies of PNNs. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: These findings highlight the importance of carefully choosing the encoding to the accuracy and decision-making strategies of PNNs. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Various convolutional neural networks (CNNs) were evaluated for their ability to detect and classify drones using spectrogram data. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: All models consistently achieved an average balanced classification accuracy of ≥ 85% at SNR > -12 dB on the development dataset. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: In the field test, the models achieved an average balance accuracy of over 80%, depending on transmitter distance and antenna direction. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: A publicly available dataset for model development was provided as part of the contributions of this paper. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper includes a comparative analysis of CNN for drone detection under low SNR conditions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An unbiased estimator of the optimal solution $\theta^*$ is provided that runs with an expected number of time-steps of order k. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The expected excess risk of the unbiased estimator is O(1/k). (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The constant behind the O notation depends on parameters of the regression and is a poly-logarithmic function of the smallest eigenvalue of H. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Numerical experiments confirm the theoretical findings of the study. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MorpMamba achieves superior parametric efficiency compared to traditional CNN and transformer models.

alignments.rationale: The publication frames the efficiency claim as an alternative to transformers (not explicitly CNNs) and emphasizes computational efficiency, shifting emphasis and narrowing the scope relative to the preprint's broader parametric-efficiency claim.

alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes morphological processing of tokens but does not mention the state-space framework explicitly, so the original claim is reframed and some aspects (state-space mention) are de-emphasized.

alignments.rationale: The publication retains the token-generation claim and adds that those tokens are processed through morphological operations, clarifying and expanding implementation detail without changing scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: AutoPV is a novel framework for the automated search and construction of photovoltaic power forecasting models based on neural architecture search technology.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The effectiveness of AutoPV is evaluated on diverse photovoltaic power forecasting tasks using a dataset from the Daqing Photovoltaic Station in China.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This work bridges the gap in applying neural architecture search to time series forecasting problems.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There are several limitations in realizing the full potential of large language models in autonomous systems.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly restate limitations specific to LLMs in autonomous systems, so the preprint limitation appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed improved two-stage octave convolutional residual blocks (IToRB) effectively enhance high and low-frequency feature extraction for screen content.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed improved two-stage octave convolutional residual blocks (IToRB) effectively enhance high and low-frequency feature extraction for screen content.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this contribution verbatim, indicating no change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The cascaded two-stage multi-scale residual blocks (CTMSRB) improve multi-scale learning and nonlinearity specifically for screen content.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The cascaded two-stage multi-scale residual blocks (CTMSRB) improve multi-scale learning and nonlinearity specifically for screen content.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this contribution with the same wording and meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method outperforms existing learned image compression methods in rate-distortion performance on screen content images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms existing learned image compression methods in rate-distortion performance on screen content images.
alignments.rationale: Both preprint and publication make the same empirical performance claim; the publication slightly rephrases but does not change the meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A diverse screen content image compression dataset (SDU-SCICD2K) has been constructed for training purposes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The constructed diverse screen content image compression dataset (SDU-SCICD2K) includes a variety of image types for training purposes.
alignments.rationale: The publication adds detail about the dataset composition (variety of image types), clarifying the original dataset construction claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The window-based attention module (WAM) captures pixel correlations effectively, particularly in high contrast regions of screen content images.
alignments.rationale: This module and its specific claim about capturing pixel correlations are not present in the preprint and therefore appear as an added publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The monograph summarizes and analyzes the current state of scientific research in interactive artificial intelligence systems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The monograph summarizes and analyzes the current state of scientific research in interactive artificial intelligence systems.
alignments.rationale: Text is effectively identical between preprint and publication, conveying the same contribution and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The monograph addresses text generation systems and diagnostics of the competitiveness of specialists.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The monograph addresses text generation systems and diagnostics of the competitiveness of specialists.
alignments.rationale: Claim wording and scope are the same in preprint and final publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings in the monograph are based on the results of the 16-th international scientific and practical conference held in October 2023.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The monograph is based on the results of the XVI international scientific and practical conference held in October 2023.
alignments.rationale: Same factual claim (roman vs numeric ordinal only); meaning and certainty unchanged.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings of the monograph will be useful to a wide range of stakeholders in the IT field and education.
alignments.rationale: This conclusion appears in the publication but has no corresponding statement in the preprint; it is an added claim about applicability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model accurately perceives and adapts to rhythmic patterns within the human perceptible range.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed neuron oscillator-based dynamic system simulates human behavior during meter perception.
alignments.rationale: Publication frames the preprint's general perception/adaptation claim in terms of a specific neuron-oscillator system and meter perception (more specific, hence clarified and slightly narrowed).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model exhibits behavior closely aligned with human rhythm interaction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments demonstrate that the proposed model can exhibit human-like reactions in common scenarios of human-machine interaction.
alignments.rationale: The publication documents experimental evidence for human-like reactions in interaction scenarios, which clarifies and supports the preprint's behavioral alignment claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduction of customized settings enhances the model's capacity to stimulate diverse human rhythmic behaviors.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model introduces two tunable parameters for local and global adjustments to emulate human-like rhythmic behavior.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the 'customized settings' as two tunable parameters, clarifying how capacity for diverse behaviors is achieved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model enables continuous learning and precise rhythm prediction through fine-tuning mechanisms and delay-feedback.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not present an equivalent claim about continuous learning, fine-tuning mechanisms, or delay-feedback, so this claim is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model is capable of transparently modeling cognitive theories related to rhythm generation in the brain.
alignments.rationale: No corresponding publication claim about modeling cognitive theories of brain rhythm generation is present; the claim appears to be dropped.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The model successfully replicates real-world rhythmic behavior in both human-machine and interhuman interactions.
alignments.rationale: This specific empirical replication claim appears in the publication but is not explicitly stated in the preprint, so it is an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The theoretical results provide insights into understanding the co-regularization relation and prove the possibility of learning transferability and discriminability abilities.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The theoretical results provide insights into understanding the co-regularization relation and prove the possibility of learning transferability and discriminability abilities.
alignments.rationale: Identical theoretical claim and certainty in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The abilities of transferability and discriminability are formulated as geometric properties between domain/cluster subspaces.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The abilities of transferability and discriminability are formulated as geometric properties between domain/cluster subspaces.
alignments.rationale: Same formulation and scope in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A geometry-oriented model is proposed for enhancing transferability and discriminability via nuclear norm optimization.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A geometry-oriented model is proposed for enhancing transferability and discriminability via nuclear norm optimization.
alignments.rationale: The contribution is presented identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive experiment results validate the effectiveness of the proposed model in empirical applications.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experiment results validate the effectiveness of the proposed model in empirical applications.
alignments.rationale: Experimental validation claim remains the same between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The geometric abilities can be sufficiently learned in the derived feasible range for the co-regularization parameters.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The geometric abilities can be sufficiently learned in the derived feasible range for the co-regularization parameters.
alignments.rationale: The technical result about feasible ranges is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Multi-Semantic Contrastive Learning (MuseCL) framework improves fine-grained urban region profiling and socioeconomic prediction.
alignments.rationale: Publication presents an unrelated method (AlphaGAT) for portfolio selection; MuseCL's urban profiling claim is absent in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: MuseCL demonstrates an average improvement of 10% in $R^2$ compared to various competitive baseline models.
alignments.rationale: The specific empirical improvement claim about MuseCL is not present in the publication, which addresses a different domain.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework effectively merges visual and textual features using a cross-modality-based attentional fusion module.
alignments.rationale: No corresponding multimodal fusion claim appears in the publication, indicating removal or complete redirection of topic.

alignments.pub_claim_text: AlphaGAT is a novel two-stage learning approach for portfolio selection that adapts to different market scenarios.
alignments.rationale: The publication introduces AlphaGAT, a claim not present in the preprint; it is an added contribution.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The first stage of AlphaGAT introduces CATimeMixer, which generates alpha factors using an advanced model with a novel loss function.
alignments.rationale: This stage-specific methodological claim is new in the published paper and absent from the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experiments on real-world market data demonstrate that AlphaGAT outperforms state-of-the-art methods.
alignments.rationale: Empirical outperforming of baselines in financial data is a publication claim not found in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The second stage of AlphaGAT employs reinforcement learning to dynamically adjust weights and integrate alpha factors into trading signals.
alignments.rationale: This reinforcement-learning-based stage is a publication-specific claim with no preprint counterpart.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The machine learning framework presented performs complete binary neutron star inference in just one second without making approximations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The machine-learning framework performs complete binary neutron star inference in just 1 second without making any approximations.
alignments.rationale: Claim is effectively identical (minor wording/formatting differences only).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach enhances multi-messenger observations by providing accurate localization even before the merger.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach enhances multi-messenger observations by providing accurate localization even before the merger.
alignments.rationale: Same claim and level of certainty in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method improves localization precision by approximately 30% compared to approximate low-latency methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method improves localization precision by around 30% compared to approximate low-latency methods.
alignments.rationale: Numerical improvement and comparison target are the same in preprint and publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework provides detailed information on luminosity distance, inclination, and masses, which can be used to prioritize expensive telescope time.
alignments.rationale: The descriptive and applied aspects of this claim are unchanged between versions.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The method scales to long signals, up to an hour in length, serving as a blueprint for data analysis for next-generation detectors.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BUSCA enhances the ability to persistently track objects missed by the detector due to occlusions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: BUSCA enhances the ability to persistently track objects missed by the detector due to occlusions. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Wording and emphasis are effectively identical between preprint and publication, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.pub_claim_text: BUSCA operates in a fully online manner without modifying past tracking results or accessing future frames. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same operational constraint and claim as the preprint with no added qualification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BUSCA demonstrates consistent performance enhancements across five different trackers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: BUSCA showcases consistent performance enhancements across five different trackers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same multi-tracker performance claim with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: BUSCA establishes a new state-of-the-art baseline across three different benchmarks. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: BUSCA establishes a new state-of-the-art baseline across three different benchmarks. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: BUSCA generates proposals based on neighboring tracks, motion, and learned tokens. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Our pattern-based approach enables automatic texture replacement for garments captured in real videos. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed per-pixel UV regression module effectively utilizes blended-weight multilayer perceptrons driven by detected discrete correspondences from cloth patterns. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method demonstrates robustness to various types of clothing, different lighting conditions, and challenging motions. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The introduction of a novel loss on the Jacobian of the UV mapping improves seam quality and reduces UV distortion. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach shows clear qualitative and quantitative improvements over baseline methods in texture replacement. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The RAPiD features exhibit rigid transformation invariance and effectively adapt to variations in point density. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: A double-nested autoencoder structure with a novel class-aware embedding objective is proposed to encode high-dimensional features into manageable voxel-wise embeddings. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Cultured meat has the potential to provide a complementary meat industry with reduced environmental, ethical, and health impacts. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning has the potential to accelerate cultured meat technology by streamlining experiments, predicting optimal results, and reducing experimentation time and resources. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of machine learning in cultured meat is in its infancy. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)

alignments.pub_claim_text: This review aims to provide the foundation necessary for both cultured meat and machine learning scientists to identify research opportunities at the intersection between cultured meat and machine learning. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The article explains how the EU's new Artificial Intelligence Act (AIA) will be implemented and enforced by various institutional bodies. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The article explains how the EU’s new Artificial Intelligence Act (AIA) may be implemented and enforced by various institutional bodies. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The article proposes a normative model of governance to ensure uniform and coordinated execution of the AIA. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The article proposes a normative governance model to ensure uniform and coordinated execution of the AIA. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The article investigates the roles of both longstanding and newly established bodies in implementing the AIA. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: EU regulations may directly dictate the structural design of institutions and indirectly request administrative capacities needed to enforce the AIA. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: EU regulations may dictate the structural design of institutions and request administrative capacities needed to enforce the AIA. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Parametric Elementary Universal Activation Function (PEUAF) is proposed as a new super-expressive activation function.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Parametric Elementary Universal Activation Function (PEUAF) demonstrates effectiveness through systematic experiments on various datasets.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the core entity (PEUAF) but emphasizes demonstrated effectiveness rather than only proposing the function, so the original claim is clarified rather than removed or revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The effectiveness of PEUAF is demonstrated through systematic and comprehensive experiments on various industrial and image datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Parametric Elementary Universal Activation Function (PEUAF) demonstrates effectiveness through systematic experiments on various datasets.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the experimental-demonstration claim with near-identical scope and certainty, so this is effectively unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Any continuous function can be approximated to any desired accuracy by a fixed-size network with a specific super-expressive activation function.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Any continuous function can be approximated to any desired accuracy by a fixed-size network with a specific super-expressive activation function.
alignments.rationale: The statement is verbatim in the publication, indicating no semantic, scope, or confidence change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The work addresses major bottlenecks in the development of super-expressive activation functions, including limited identification and peculiar forms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The work addresses major bottlenecks in the development of super-expressive activation functions, including limited identification and peculiar forms.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this contribution claim with equivalent wording and certainty, so it is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed edge-AI enabled detector achieves a mean average precision (mAP) of 95.1% and an F1-score of 94.2%.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed FCOS-Lite detector achieves a mean average precision (mAP) of 95.1% and an F1-score of 94.2%, outperforming other state-of-the-art detectors.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed gradient weighting loss function and CIOU loss function enhance the performance of compact edge-AI detectors without incurring additional inference costs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A gradient weighting loss function for classification and a CIOU loss function for localization are proposed to mitigate reduced accuracy in compact edge-AI detectors.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The knowledge distillation scheme improves the performance of the FCOS-Lite detector while maintaining a compact model size.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The innovative approach leveraging edge-AI technology achieves high performance and efficiency in a memory-constrained environment.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the specific knowledge-distillation claim into a broader statement about the overall approach achieving high performance in constrained environments, so the specific mechanism is generalized and the scope broadened.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DeepBoW model is an efficient and interpretable relevance architecture for Chinese e-commerce.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DeepBoW model is an efficient and interpretable relevance architecture for Chinese e-commerce.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model offers a highly explainable and interventionable representation compared to popular dense distributed representations.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DeepBoW model offers a highly explainable and interventionable representation compared to dense distributed representations.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The online efficiency of the DeepBoW model is superior to the most efficient inner product form of dense representation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model achieves competitive performance while providing much more efficient online inference compared to other models.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The innovative lighting system changes specific target colors while keeping the lights appearing naturally white.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The system achieves unique color variations by precisely controlling the spectral power distribution (SPD) of illumination and harnessing metamerism.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The system calculates the optimal SPDs of illumination for given materials to intensively induce metamerism.

alignments.pub_claim_text: We present a data-, mesh-, and grid-free solution for elastic simulation for any object in any geometric representation undergoing large, nonlinear deformations.

alignments.pub_claim_text: We define a simulator atop a common interface that reduces every standard geometric representation to an occupancy function queried at any point in space.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The weights in our implicit neural network are trained to learn physically significant motions in the object via random perturbations.
